items.correct: Meaning conveyed beyond what is literally said — generated when speakers obey or deliberately flout Grice's maxims. 'Some students passed' implicates 'not all', via the maxim of quantity.
items.misconception: Implicature is the same as the literal meaning of a sentence.
items.random_wrong: Implicature is a kind of grammatical mistake.
items.why: Implicature is precisely the gap between said and meant. Students conflate literal and intended meaning, missing why pragmatics is needed at all — much of communication is implied, not stated.

items.q: What is the difference between overt and covert prestige?
items.correct: Overt prestige is the high status of standard varieties recognised by society (RP, Standard English); covert prestige is the hidden status of non-standard varieties within particular groups, signalling solidarity or toughness.
items.misconception: Only standard language has prestige; non-standard varieties have none.
items.random_wrong: Overt prestige is loud speech and covert prestige is quiet speech.
items.why: Covert prestige explains why people maintain non-standard varieties despite social stigma — they carry value within their communities. Students assume prestige is single-dimensional, missing why language change isn't all toward the standard.

items.correct: The smallest unit of meaning in a language — a word like 'unhappiness' has three morphemes: un-, happy, and -ness.
items.random_wrong: A morpheme is a sound that has no meaning.
items.why: Morphemes are units of meaning; syllables are units of sound. 'Strength' is one syllable but two morphemes (strong + th). Students conflate the two units.

items.q: What does the L in PEEL stand for?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Link. “List” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Which reading skill hunts specific facts?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Scanning. “Skimming” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: How many letters in PEEL paragraph structure?
items.why: Exact answer: 4 letters. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
